Sometime later, Xu Xuan retrieved the Primordial Phoenix Heart and the Incandescent Dragon's Essence, handing it over to Su Yang. "As for the treasure you want, you can either wait for me to retrieve it, or you can retrieve it yourself. You should know its location, right?" Su Yang said after putting away the two treasures.

"I'm not in a rush to acquire it, so I'll wait for you to give it to me. Moreover, it won't be the same if I grabbed it myself," she said.

"Alright."

chapters.title: Chapter 1087 Fiery Yang Cavern
chapters.body:
Chapter 1087 Fiery Yang Cavern

Chapter 1087 Fiery Yang Cavern

"So, what are you going to do now, Su Yang?" Xu Xuan asked him as he prepared to leave.

Su Yang popped an appearance-altering pill inside his mouth, returning to being Xiao Yang, and said, "I'm going to return to the Boundless Yin Yang Sect and work my way into their Eternal Yin Yang Cave."

"The Eternal Yin Yang Cave, huh? So your goal is Celestial Yang Qi. I guess things are different now that you can cultivate Celestial Qi."

"Huh? You knew?" Su Yang looked at her with a slightly surprised face.

"I can taste it in your Yang Qi, albeit very little—almost nonexistent. How did you manage to do that when even your old body couldn't achieve such a thing?"

"I think it has something to do with my cultivation technique."

"You changed it? Don't people normally go for something they're already used to?" "Yes, but this one is simply too good. I'm sure you've heard of it before. It's called the Celestial Body Refining Scripture."

"What! That's the Heavenly Emperor's cultivation technique!" Xu Xuan cried out loud, her face filled with shock.

Su Yang smiled at her normal reaction and said, "With this technique, I'll not only be able to surpass my previous limit, but I can finally aim for the true peak of cultivation—Celestial God!"

"Good luck with that. Not even the Heavenly Emperor could reach that level."

Su Yang chuckled and said, "And does the Heavenly Emperor have the Celestial Constitution?"

"You're going to build another Celestial Constitution? Do you remember how much chaos that caused in the past?" Xu Xuan rubbed her eyes.

Su Yang had incited chaos and offended countless people in his past efforts to acquire his Celestial Constitution. Xu Xuan found it hard to imagine witnessing such turmoil unfold once again.

"However… if you're able to acquire the Celestial Constitution… even I cannot imagine how far you'll go."

"Unfortunately, I still have a lot of things to do before I focus on that." Su Yang sighed.

Sometime later, Su Yang and Xu Xuan kissed each other one last time before Xu Xuan sent him back to the front of the hotel, where she initially kidnapped him. 'I'll be seeing you again soon, Su Yang…' Xu Xuan's voice resounded inside his head.

While Su Yang returned to the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, Xu Xuan returned to the Autumn Phantom Manor.

"What took you so long to return, Elder Sister?"

"You said you'd only be gone for a month at most…"

"Did you find that rude mortal? Did you teach him a lesson?"

"What about the Phantom Cat, Xiao Rong? Did you convince her to join us?"

Bombarded by questions, Xu Xuan shouted, "Shut up for a moment!"

Once the place was dead silent, Xu Xuan spoke, "From now on, you'll respect that person as if he's your father."

"What?! That mortal?!"

"No way! Did he do something weird to you, Elder Sister?!"

The Ghost Cats were in disbelief.

"..."

Even if she would entrust her own life in their hands, Xu Xuan didn't dare to expose Su Yang's identity to them just yet, fearing that one of them might accidentally reveal his identity since they are still young and inexperienced.

"Enough! I will not allow anyone to badmouth him, even if it's you girls! You may not know this, but he saved my life before. If it weren't for him, I wouldn't be here right now!" Xu Xuan said to them in a stern voice.

"As for Xiao Rong, she's in good hands, so you don't need to worry about her."

The little ones were speechless. They couldn't fathom how a mortal could save Xu Xuan's life, but there was no reason for Xu Xuan to lie to them.

"Since he saved your life, I guess we can give him some face and show some respect the next time he visits…"

"Is that why you gave him the family medallion?"

"It makes sense now…"

"Elder Sister, are you going to leave again soon?"

Xu Xuan shook her head and said, "No, I no longer need to leave since I have already resolved my business outside. I will return to managing the Autumn Phantom Manor with the rest of you."

"Really? That is great!"

The little ones were ecstatic to hear this news.

Meanwhile, after he returned to the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, Su Yang reported his missions.

"This is the fifth high-priority mission you've completed in a row. Just how do you do it…?" The Sect Elder was left speechless by Su Yang's achievements, something that even Core Disciples would have difficulty accomplishing.

"I just got lucky." Su Yang smiled.

"Whatever. Here are your rewards."

The Sect Elder handed Su Yang some points and spirit stones for his work.

Su Yang returned to his living quarters afterward.

'I still have two days before I enter the Fiery Yang Cavern. Let's use this time to prepare myself.'

Su Yang looked at his spatial ring, which contained the Primordial Phoenix Heart and the Incandescent Dragon's Essence. Although he told Xu Xuan that he wouldn't use them anytime soon, it wasn't entirely true.

"Welcome back, Manager!" Mei Xing greeted him at the door with a bright smile on her face.

"Congratulations on your breakthrough." Su Yang smiled back.

"Thank you!"

"Manager, welcome back." Mei Ying came out after their voices.

"You also had a breakthrough. Congratulations."

"This is all thanks to the Yang Qi you left behind." Mei Ying smiled.

"Anyway, I have some business within the sect in two days, so I'll be leaving again soon. I won't be gone for long this time, though. You guys can return to your cultivation."

"Okay."

Su Yang took out the Primordial Phoenix Heart and examined it intently for several moments. Once prepared, he carefully sliced off a tiny piece, no larger than a grain of sand. Then, he retrieved his cauldron and proceeded to refine a pill with it.

"This should be good enough." He nodded with a satisfied look on his face after examining the pill.

Two days passed in the blink of an eye, and Chun Hua showed up at his house early in the morning.

chapters.title: Chapter 1088 Peak Of Sovereign Spirit Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 1088 Peak Of Sovereign Spirit Realm

Chapter 1088 Peak of Sovereign Spirit Realm

"I hope you've prepared yourself, Disciple Xiao." Chun Hua greeted him with a mischievous smile.

"I'm plenty prepared. I hope the same could be said about you."

"I have tripled checked the schedule and even visited the Fiery Yang Cavern earlier. It's completely empty there. You're good to go."

"Let's go then."

Su Yang and Chun Hua made their way to the Fiery Yang Cavern shortly after.

In the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, the Yang Nourishment Peak stood as a notable area, featuring places like the Fiery Yang Cavern. It was among the most populated areas within the sect, primarily because of its unique purpose. Male disciples frequented this location to enhance their Yang Qi, making it a hub of activity and training.

Before they entered the Yang Nourishment Peak, Su Yang and Chun Hua consumed an appearance-altering pill just in case.

Sometime later, they arrived at the Fiery Yang Cavern. The location was somewhere between the middle area and the peak of the Yang Nourishment Peak, where the Profound Qi was more abundant than the lower areas.

The Fiery Yang Cavern itself appeared to be an ordinary mountain at a glance, but there were hundreds of powerful arrays and formations that protected and maintained the place.

There were four entrances to the Fiery Yang Cavern, and all of them were opened. Typically, there would be guards at each entrance, but since the place was closed and disciples were prohibited from entering, it was completely empty at this moment.

"Alright, go ahead. You have 14 days inside, but come out on the 13th day to be safe. I'll keep watch out here just in case, so you can cultivate in peace." Chun Hua gestured for him to enter the Fiery Yang Cavern.

The Boundless Yin Yang Sect enforced strict rules prohibiting certain behaviors among its disciples, especially engagements involving cultivation resources. If caught engaging in these forbidden activities, the consequences were severe, almost invariably resulting in expulsion from the sect. However, Su Yang was not new to breaking sect rules. In fact, he has broken nearly every sect rule in his past life. Moreover, there was a good chance he wouldn't face expulsion even if he were to be caught due to the Yang Qi that he supplied the sect.

Without an ounce of worry, Su Yang confidently entered the Fiery Yang Cavern. Seeing Su Yang's relaxed behavior left Chun Hua dumbfounded.

'Can't he act nervous even a little? It's almost as if he's eager to break sect rules. And isn't he worried that I might be tricking him with a trap?' Chun Hua wondered inwardly.

Su Yang had a lot of enemies within the Outer Court due to his massage business, and he had already experienced foul play before, so it would only be natural for Su Yang to doubt everything.

Inside the Fiery Yang Cavern, the walls were adorned with beautiful, glowing red flowers. These flowers emitted a unique scent that stirred the Yang Qi inside Su Yang's body, causing his little brother to awaken.

Once he reached several hundred meters into the cavern, Su Yang was greeted by a large cenote filled with steaming hot water. Just by breathing in the steam, Su Yang could feel his Yang Qi strengthening.

However, Su Yang didn't stop there and continued walking deeper into the cavern.

The Fiery Yang Cavern was divided into three distinct areas, each designed to offer different benefits to the cultivators who trained there.

The outer area, which Su Yang had just encountered, was intended for beginners. Here, the waters were imbued with properties that significantly increased one's Yang Qi recovery rate.

Due to his low cultivation base, it would be an ideal spot for Su Yang if it weren't for the fact that he already had endless Yang Qi.

In his past life, he was born with a unique body that gave him inexhaustible Yang Qi, but in this life, he had a powerful technique that allowed him to recover his Yang Qi so quickly that it was essentially limitless.

The second area of the Fiery Yang Cavern was smaller than the first, designed specifically for those in the Divine Realm. This section focused on enhancing both the quantity and quality of one's Yang Qi.

Su Yang was only at the Sovereign Spirit Realm, so cultivating there would be incredibly painful for him. However, he was in a hurry to increase his cultivation, so he decided to cultivate there.

The third and innermost area was typically reserved for Sect Elders. This section was known for its extremely potent effects, so much so that even Core Disciples found it unbearable, and only the most talented of Core Disciples would dare to enter. After removing his clothes, Su Yang submerged his perfect body in the steaming water. The second his body touched the water, it began absorbing Profound Qi automatically, and his cultivation base began to increase at a significant rate.

However, it was excruciatingly painful for Su Yang, feeling like he was bathing in needles that constantly pricked his body. Yet, there was nothing on his face that indicated he was in pain. If one were to look at him at this moment, one would think he was actually relaxed.

After spending half a day cultivating in the Fiery Yang Cavern, Su Yang's cultivation had a breakthrough, but he didn't stop and continued.

Three days later, Su Yang's cultivation reached the peak of the Sovereign Spirit Realm.

At that point, Su Yang stopped his cultivation and emerged from the water. However, he still had ten days in the Fiery Yang Cavern and no plans to leave any time soon.

His gaze shifted towards the third and most challenging area of the cavern, reserved for Sect Elders. A determined expression settled on his handsome face as he began walking toward it, ready to test his limits and, perhaps, push beyond them.

Upon arriving at the third area, Su Yang retrieved the pill he had prepared using the Primordial Phoenix Heart and stared at it nervously.

chapters.title: Chapter 1089 Breakthroughs
chapters.body:
Chapter 1089 Breakthroughs

Chapter 1089 Breakthroughs

The third area of Fiery Yang Cavern was already challenging enough for Su Yang despite his breakthrough. Yet, he also planned on consuming a pill that could easily kill someone at his current cultivation.

"Even with my heaven-defying cultivation technique, this is incredibly reckless and risky. Unfortunately, I do not have the time to take things slowly."

He closed his eyes and took a long, deep breath.

"Ahhh… the things I do for you girls…"

Su Yang tossed the pill into his mouth and leaped into the water. Almost instantly, his body began to expand, as though it were on the brink of bursting from the overwhelming surge of Profound Qi from both the water and the pill.

Veins bulged across his entire body, then began to burst, spraying his blood in every direction.

Once his entire body was drenched in blood, it began healing at an astonishing speed, quickly returning to its original state. However, the moment he fully recovered, his body was damaged again, restarting the cycle almost instantly.

This relentless cycle continued for several hours until, suddenly, Su Yang's body was engulfed in flames that seared his skin and hair away in an instant.

An unimaginable pain surged through Su Yang's entire being, as if he had been thrown into the heart of the sun with a thousand swords piercing his body.

Su Yang had plenty of painful experiences in his past, including being tortured for months, but even his most painful moments couldn't be compared to the excruciating pain he was experiencing at this moment.

Furthermore, the pain only worsened as time passed. He felt as if he was on the verge of collapsing, but he desperately held onto his consciousness by the thread.

'If I lose consciousness and fail to control the Profound Qi even for a split second, my body will explode, and I will perish!'

While Su Yang's mind was fully occupied by the pain, his body was experiencing a profound transformation from his Yang Qi to the foundation of his physique.

Every cell in his body was being reformed and enhanced. His bones grew stronger, and the quality of his Yang Qi increased. Yet, the most significant transformation was in his physique, which had long stagnated due to a shortage of resources.

Over the next several days, the pain and effects of the pill combined with the Fiery Yang Cavern continuously increased, but Su Yang's endurance also became stronger—to the point where he could cultivate with a calm appearance. Of course, his body was still experiencing excruciating pain.

Meanwhile, Chun Hua noticed a figure wearing a specific robe for the disciplinary squad approaching the Fiery Yang Cavern.

'He must be here for a random inspection. How annoying.'

Chun Hua quickly approached this individual before he could use his spiritual sense on the Fiery Yang Cavern, which would expose Su Yang.

"Excuse me! You're from the disciplinary squad, right?" Chun Hua approached him with a nervous appearance.

"Yes, that's right. Is something wrong?"

"Good! There's an issue at the Yang Spiritual Waterfall that needs your attention before it gets out of hand!" Chun Hua said with urgency in her tone.

"The Yang Spiritual Waterfall? What a pain in the ass. There's always trouble there. Anyway, I'll go take a look. Thank you for warning me." The disciple didn't find Chun Hua's actions suspicious and didn't bother questioning her further, as the Yang Spiritual Waterfall was known to be a rowdy place where disciples frequently fought for the best spots. "Thank you, Senior Brother!"

Once the disciple left, Chun Hua retrieved a communication jade slip and spoke to it, "Go cause some trouble at the Yang Spiritual Waterfall. Do it right now."

"What kind of trouble?" A voice responded.

"I don't care."

"As you wish."

Chun Hua returned to guarding Su Yang afterward.

'I wonder how strong he'll be after coming out? If he cultivated in the second area, he should be able to reach the peak of Sovereign Spirit Realm in 13 days.'

Eleven days have passed since Su Yang entered the Fiery Yang Cavern. Chun Hua would occasionally hear screams of pain coming from the place.

On the twelfth day, Su Yang emerged from the water, now entirely tainted by his blood, transforming it into a pool of crimson.

His already flawless body had transcended perfection, and his aura was now countless times more formidable.

His physique, which hadn't advanced for some time, had now reached the astonishing peak of the Divine Spirit Realm. His cultivation, which had only recently reached the peak of the Sovereign Spirit Realm, soared past the Divine Spirit Realm, shattered through the Seven Mortal Spirit Realm, and ascended into the Five Divine Realm, reaching the first level of the Divine Origin Realm.

'It's been a while since I last experienced such significant breakthroughs. The effects are better than anticipated, and even my Celestial Qi increased slightly. I'll have to thank Xu Xuan properly the next time I see her.'

However, to avoid raising too many eyebrows, Su Yang suppressed his cultivation to the first level of the Divine Spirit Realm.

Though he still had a day remaining at the Fiery Yang Cavern, Su Yang decided to leave early, realizing it would no longer provide him with significant benefits.

Before leaving, Su Yang tossed a pill into the water to accelerate the purification process of the Fiery Yang Cavern. Although the cavern naturally cleansed itself of impurities, he worried it wouldn't be quick enough.

Once the pill dissolved, it took less than ten minutes for the water to return to its pristine and pure state. However, the depleted spiritual energy and potency of the Fiery Yang Cavern couldn't be restored so quickly. In just twelve days, Su Yang had exhausted at least a decade's worth of the cavern's vitality.

'The cavern's drastic decrease in vitality during its resting period will definitely raise some eyebrows, but there's nothing I can do about it.' Su Yang shrugged and left the Fiery Yang Cavern, feeling like he'd just experienced a rebirth.

chapters.title: Chapter 1090 Delayed Heavenly Tribulation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1090 Delayed Heavenly Tribulation

Chapter 1090 Delayed Heavenly Tribulation

When Chun Hua noticed Su Yang coming out of the Fiery Yang Cavern, she examined his body before approaching him.

"First level of Divine Spirit Realm, huh? You have slightly surpassed my expectations, but why did you come out already? You still have an entire day inside."

"I won't benefit anymore from the place, so I left early," he said.

Chun Hua couldn't believe what Su Yang had just said and quickly responded, "Won't benefit you…? What kind of nonsense is that? The Fiery Yang Cavern is beneficial for anyone below the Five Divine Realm, and even for those whose cultivation is above that level, it can still enhance their Yang Qi!"

Su Yang shrugged and said, "If given enough time, sure. However, I only had a day left at best. I don't want to risk getting exposed over such minor improvements."

"I guess that makes sense…" Chun Hua mumbled after hearing his explanation.

Su Yang continued speaking, "Anyway, I am going to leave now. I'll contact you later so I can thank you properly." "Huh? What do you mean?" Chun Hua wore a puzzled look on her face.

Su Yang smiled and said, "I find it unlikely that you would put yourself at so much risk just to cultivate with me. There's something else you want from me, right? You can tell me about it later. As long as it isn't out of my abilities, I'll help you."

A profound smile appeared on Chun Hua's face, but she didn't reveal anything else and only said, "Then I will be waiting for you."

"Then I'll take my leave now."

"You seem to be in a hurry to leave. Do you have an appointment with someone?" she suddenly asked.

"Yeah, I do."

Before Chun Hua could say anything else, Su Yang flew away.

Chun Hua didn't leave the Fiery Yang Cavern until Su Yang's silhouette completely disappeared from the horizon.

Once he was gone, Chun Hua turned to look at the Fiery Yang Cavern and entered the cave Su Yang had cultivated in.

"Wow, he exhausted so much vitality from the Fiery Yang Cavern in just twelve days. There's no way he could've absorbed this much from cultivating in the second area. He must've cultivated in the third area. How bold and reckless."

Chun Hua didn't stay in the Fiery Yang Cavern for long and left almost as soon as she entered.

Meanwhile, Su Yang searched for a secluded peak in the Boundless Yin Yang Sect.

'Due to how fast I broke through to the Divine Origin Realm, and with the formations in the Fiery Yang Cavern, my Heavenly Tribulation was delayed. However, I can feel it quickly approaching.'

Typically, to transition from the Seven Mortal Spirit Realm to the Five Divine Realm, a cultivator must face and endure a Heavenly Tribulation. However, Su Yang forcefully broke through with the aid of a powerful treasure, and his presence was partially concealed by the formations within the Fiery Yang Cavern, causing the Heavenly Tribulation to be delayed.

Heavenly Tribulations cannot be avoided and are inevitable. Furthermore, if delayed, the Heavenly Tribulation becomes even more powerful, increasing the risk and challenge for the cultivator. For this reason, most cultivators choose to face their Heavenly Tribulations as soon as possible.

Within the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, there are empty peaks designated for this exact purpose. Once Su Yang found a suitable spot for his Heavenly Tribulation, he swiftly set up several formations around the area to conceal his presence and reduce the chances of being disturbed.

After all that was done, Su Yang sat in the lotus position and waited for the Heavenly Tribulation to occur.

A few minutes later, the temperature and atmosphere on Su Yang's peak changed dramatically. The clouds above him darkened, swirling ominously as if a powerful thunderstorm was imminent.

Su Yang looked up and smiled, "What's taking so long? You're just a minor tribulation. Get this over with, and stop wasting my precious time."

BANG!

As if in response to Su Yang's provocation, the dark clouds unleashed a lightning strike as thick as a mature tree directly at him, accompanied by a deafening thunderclap that rippled throughout the Boundless Yin Yang Sect.

"Someone's facing a Heavenly Tribulation in our sect? Is it a disciple or an elder that's making the breakthrough?"

The disciples were curious about this individual's identity, but they knew better than to disturb someone undergoing a tribulation. Whether it was a rule of the sect or a general rule among cultivators, interrupting another's tribulation was strictly frowned upon. freeωebnovēl.c૦m

However, as long as they didn't interfere with the cultivator, it was acceptable to observe the tribulation. Consequently, many experts in the sect used their spiritual senses to watch from a distance.

"An Outer Court disciple? I don't recognize him."

"Wait… he's already at the Divine Origin Realm? But the Heavenly Tribulation hasn't ended yet! Did he manage to breakthrough before the tribulation could even begin?" "He's enduring the Heavenly Tribulation as if it's nothing! Who is this genius, and how is he only an Outer Court disciple at that cultivation?!"

The spectators were shocked by what they were witnessing.

Furthermore, Su Yang had changed his appearance before the tribulation began to avoid being recognized. He also lifted the suppression on his cultivation, revealing his true level to everyone watching.

The Heavenly Tribulation ended after ninety-nine lightning strikes, and Su Yang emerged from it without a single scratch on his body.

The spectators were eager to approach Su Yang and learn more about him, but he quickly left the scene. They attempted to track him with their spiritual senses, but Su Yang's movement technique had reached a new level after his breakthrough, enabling him to disappear like a ghost.

"Damn! He got away!"

"Who the hell was that, anyway?"

"He must've been in a disguise to avoid attention. I highly doubt he was an Outer Court disciple, too."

"He just entered the Divine Origin Realm, so he must be an Inner Court disciple at the very least. We'll find out his identity if anyone attempts to become a Core Disciple in the near future."

With their prey having disappeared, the spectators departed soon after.

chapters.title: Chapter 1091 Chun Hua’S Request
chapters.body:
Chapter 1091 Chun Hua’S Request

Chapter 1091 Chun Hua's Request

After dealing with his Heavenly Tribulation, Su Yang returned to his residence at the Outcast Peak.

"Hey, Xiao Yang. Haven't seen you around in a while. Been busy?" Li Sang was leaving the Outcast Peak just as Su Yang returned.

He nodded, "Yes, I've been busy with sect missions."

"Is that—Hmm?! Wait a minute! Your cultivation! How the heck did you reach the Divine Spirit Realm in such a short amount of time?!"

Su Yang showed a humble smile on his face and said, "I had a fortuitous encounter encounter outside the sect while doing my mission."

"Outside?! But you're only an Outer Court disciple!" Li Sang exclaimed. Then he remembered rumors about an Outer Court disciple who had received special permission from the Sect Leader to leave the sect for certain missions.

"Y-you must be that Outer Court disciple from the rumors! I can't believe it! How did you manage to do that?! I also want to leave the sect!"

"You wouldn't be able to replicate it even if I told you." Su Yang shook his head.

"Anyway, I'll see you around."

Su Yang swiftly entered his living quarters, leaving Li Sang speechless.

"Welcome back, Manager." Mei Ying greeted him.

"Now that I have some time, want to cultivate?" Su Yang wrapped his arm around her slender waist and said.

"Of course." Mei Ying nodded with a bashful smile.

Su Yang proceeded the next two days cultivating with Mei Ying, further boosting her cultivation.

Ever since Su Yang gave Mei Ying a pill that transformed her constitution and enhanced her talents, her progress had accelerated dramatically, rivaling even those considered geniuses in the Boundless Yin Yang Sect.

However, the thing that assisted her cultivation the most was Su Yang's potent Yang Qi. Now that he strengthened his Yang Qi even further, Mei Ying also reaped his benefits.

"When do you plan on improving Mei Xing's talents? And is there a reason you only improved mine?" Mei Ying suddenly asked him.

He nodded and said, "I want her to experience what it's like to cultivate without any talents so that she doesn't grow up to be too complacent, but the most important reason is that she's too young to consume the pill. Once she's of age, I'll reform her talents."

"I see… thank you for everything…"

"Don't mention it."

The next day, Su Yang left the Outcast Peak to travel to the inner court.

His reason for going there? To investigate Chun Hua's identity.

While he was grateful to her for what she had done for him thus far, she was simply too suspicious to ignore.

Once he was at the inner court, Su Yang asked around about Chun Hua under the guise of trying to court her. Of course, he disguised himself as an Inner Court disciple before doing so to make it more convenient.

"Senior apprentice-sister Chun? She's an introvert and doesn't speak much."

"Junior apprentice-sister Chun is a bashful but determined girl. She likes to read and play the zither."

"You want to court Sister Chun Hua? But she's already taken."

After spending a whole day gathering information on Chun Hua, Su Yang realized that she was nothing like how others perceived her.

"Introvert? Bashful? That's the complete opposite of how she appears before me!"

Although the stark difference in her character was suspicious, it didn't concern Su Yang too much. After all, most people behave differently depending on who they are with, and some take that to the extreme.

At the end of the day, Su Yang went to find Chun Hua in her living quarters.

"You're here much faster than I anticipated. Did you miss me that much?" Chun Hua joked immediately after answering the door.

"I don't like to owe debts," he said.

Chun Hua gestured for him to enter, "Please, come inside."

However, Su Yang didn't move and said, "Are you sure? What if your partner shows up?"

Chun Hua chuckled, "You asked around about me? If you're that interested, you could've just asked me directly. I don't have any problems with that. Anyway, in response to your worries, we broke up shortly before I approached you, so you don't need to worry about my partner."

"Is that so?" Su Yang didn't say anything else and entered her living quarters.

Once inside, Chun Hua led him straight to her bedroom and gestured for him to sit on the bed.

However, Su Yang sat in her chair instead.

"You're no fun." Chun Hua sighed as she lay on the bed.

"So, what do you want from me?" he went straight to the topic.

"Why don't you take a guess? Just one is fine."

Su Yang pondered for a moment before responding, "Since you went out of your way to risk expulsion sneaking me into the Fiery Yang Cavern, I guess you want me to do something as risky, if not even riskier."

"Wrong!" she instantly responded.

"While it's risky, it's not in the way you're thinking. I want you to participate in the Yang Qi King."

"Yang Qi King?" Su Yang raised an eyebrow.

'Is this why she led me to the Fiery Yang Cavern? To improve my Yang Qi and enter this competition?' he wondered inwardly.

"Why would you want me to participate in something meant for Inner Court and Core Disciples?"

"Because I think you'll be able to win it. Of course, even if you don't, that's completely fine. I just want you to participate in it."

"And how does that benefit you?"

"It doesn't. I just want to see how the others will react if someone who just joined the sect wins the competition. Of course, this will put you in the spotlight."

"Attention, huh? This must be the risk that you're speaking of."

"You don't want it? Even if you want to remain lowkey, you're already past that point, and you're someone who will inevitably attract attention. After all, that is the kind of person you are."

Su Yang smiled and said, "If you'd made this request before I snuck into the Fiery Yang Cavern, I would've declined. However, now that I am more capable, I have no plans of staying lowkey."

"Does that mean…"

He nodded, "Yes, I'll participate in the Yang Qi King."

chapters.title: Chapter 1092 It Won’T Bite Unless You Want It To
chapters.body:
Chapter 1092 It Won’T Bite Unless You Want It To

Chapter 1092 It Won’t Bite Unless You Want It To

"That's great!" Chun Hua was very delighted when Su Yang agreed to participate in the Yang King Qi.

Although Su Yang didn't mention it, he was already interested in the Yang Qi King when he initially heard about it from Elder Yao. Even if Chun Hua hadn't requested Su Yang to join, he would've probably participated anyway.

Chun Hua continued speaking, "The Yang Qi King has already begun accepting submissions, and every disciple has thirty days to hand in their Yang Qi if they wish to participate, so you have another twenty-three days to submit your Yang Qi. Most disciples wait until the last day to submit their Yang Qi to get the most out of it."

"Is that so?"

Upon learning this information, Su Yang decided to submit his Yang Qi as soon as possible.

'I should adjust my Yang Qi so that it's not too suspicious since the sect already has information on my Yang Qi.'

It'd raise eyebrows if he suddenly submitted Yang Qi that far surpassed what he'd given them a few months ago. Moreover, Su Yang still had to decide if he wanted to win the competition or just participate. freewebnσvel.cѳm

The rewards might be highly tempting to Inner Court disciples and even some Core Disciples, but the Golden Sun Pill was useless to Su Yang. The Scorching Yang Sauna, although beneficial as it was essentially an upgraded version of the Fiery Yang Cavern, wouldn't provide him with significant advantages due to his high cultivation base. As for his Yang Qi, it was already at a level where only extremely powerful treasures or places like the Eternal Yin Yang Cave could enhance its quality further.

'If I am going to participate, I should definitely try to win it. Otherwise, it would be a waste of time.'

Su Yang made a swift decision. He was someone who refused to lose, no matter what he was doing or how insignificant it might be.

"Do you have any information on those who will definitely be participating? For instance, who is most likely to win the competition?" Su Yang suddenly asked.

Chun Hua pondered for a moment before responding, "Hard to say since there is always surprise participation from those who claimed they wouldn't participate. If you want an accurate answer, you will have to wait until the very last day. After all, we'll only know the participants at the end of the competition when the results are out."

"You want me to wait until the last day for what? Do you have a way of acquiring a list of all participants?"

Even if he waited until the last day, they wouldn't know who had participated until the results were announced. The only way Chun Hua could have such information would be if she somehow obtained the participant list on the very last day, right before the submission deadline.

"Yes, I know someone who can acquire the participant list," she responded confidently.

Su Yang smiled and said, "No, it's fine. I don't want to owe you anymore."

"Then what are you going to do?"

"I'll just have to make sure to submit Yang Qi that won't lose to even the top disciple in the sect."

Chun Hua's jaw dropped, and she said, "How are you so confident? Even if you cultivated in the Fiery Yang Cavern for a hundred years, I doubt you'll be able to defeat the top Core Disciples."

"I was hoping you would get lucky with this year's participants and somehow win. I don't really expect you to win. You know that, right?"

"No, I don't." Su Yang narrowed his gaze on her and said, "In fact, I'm sure you expect me to win."

"..."

Chun Hua fell silent at his claims.

"Whatever. If you say so."

She eventually shrugged it off.

"Anyway, you got any more questions about the Yang Qi King?"

He shook his head.

"No, not really."

"Then how about a quick cultivation session with me before you leave? I want to experience for myself what makes you so confident that you'll be able to win," Chun Hua licked her lips.

"Quick?" Su Yang smiled. "I'm not sure you mean what you say."

"Don't mind the small details," she chuckled.

Su Yang didn't refuse and spent the next three days cultivating with Chun Hua.

"Where do I go to submit my Yang Qi?" Su Yang asked her right before leaving her living quarters.

"You can give it to any Sect Elder," she answered.

"Thanks."

After leaving Chun Hua's place, Su Yang immediately made his way to Elder Yao's living quarter.

Sometime later, he knocked on Elder Yao's door.

Elder Yao appeared before him shortly after and, without saying anything to him, extended her hand out, almost as if she was expecting something from him.

Su Yang knew she wanted his friend's Yang Qi, so he handed her three bottles. After receiving the bottles, Elder Yao said, "See you next time."

And she began closing the door.

However, Su Yang moved his foot forward, preventing her from closing the door.

"What do you want? I'm extremely busy nowadays and don't have the time to entertain you," she sighed.

"I'm here to submit my Yang Qi for the Yang Qi King competition."

"...Seriously?" Elder Yao's face became dumbfounded.

"Seriously."

"Why are you asking me?"

This was the first time a disciple approached her for submission.

"You're the only Sect Elder I'm close to," he casually said. "Why? You can't do it? I was told I could submit it to any Sect Elder."

Elder Yao rubbed her eyes and sighed, "Fine. Get in here."

She opened the door to let him inside.

After Su Yang closed the door behind him, Elder Yao retrieved an empty vial and handed it to him.

"Fill the vial with your Yang Qi. I'll need to see you do it." Elder Yao said.

Despite the seemingly ridiculous request, Elder Yao spoke in a nonchalant tone, as if it were a completely standard procedure.

After all, if they accepted pre-prepared submissions, there was a risk that the participant could cheat by using someone else's Yang Qi. Thus, the participant must submit their Yang Qi on the spot before a witness.

"Sure."

Su Yang didn't have any issues and swiftly loosened his robes, revealing his already awakened dragon.

Elder Yao's eyes widened, and she even jumped back a little when she saw Su Yang's large sword, as if she had been jumpscared by it.

Seeing her surprising reaction, Su Yang teased, "Don't worry, it won't bite unless you want it to."

chapters.title: Chapter 1094 Elder Yao’S Assistance(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1094 Elder Yao’S Assistance(2)

Chapter 1094 Elder Yao's Assistance(2)

"Elder Yao, do you mind doing it from the front? It's a little hard to see your beautiful breasts from this angle." Su Yang said a minute later.

"..."

Elder Yao didn't respond, but she moved in front of him a moment later, even kneeling before his rod without being asked to.

'It's so close…' Elder Yao swallowed nervously as she stared at the massive dragon before her.

Su Yang's lower sword was so close to her that she could clearly smell its scent. As an alchemist, her sense of smell was far more acute than that of the average cultivator, making the experience even more intense.

'Why does it smell so good? Do all men smell this good down here?' Elder Yao wondered inwardly as she entered a trance.

Her hand movements were so smooth that they seemed almost natural, as if she had been doing this for decades.

While in her trance, Elder Yao instinctively started using her other hand to massage Su Yang's family jewels.

Su Yang released even more transparent liquid. There was so much that they began flowing down to Elder Yao's hands like syrup from trees.

'What a weird texture… I wonder if they taste as good as they look…' Elder Yao swallowed forcefully.

When her curiosity exceeded her self-restraint, Elder Yao subconsciously moved her face closer to Su Yang's sword.

However, just as she opened her mouth and began sticking her tongue out, the dragon before her abruptly released a powerful stream of hot liquid.

This liquid was different from the transparent dew. It was white and filled with Profound Qi.

"Ah?!"

Elder Yao jerked her head back when Su Yang's Yang Qi shot all over her face, with some even entering her mouth.

'It entered my mouth!'

Just as Elder Yao began fuming angrily, she tasted the Yang Qi.

'And it's delicious!' she cried inwardly.

"Why did you release your Yang Qi without warning me?! I couldn't even collect it because of you!" she shouted at him a moment later.

"But I did warn you, Elder Yao… In fact, I warned you several times, but you didn't respond, and I could only hold it in for so long…" Su Yang shook his head.

Elder Yao's face became dumbfounded after hearing his words.

'Was I really so absorbed in jerking him off that I failed to hear his warning?!' she screamed internally after realizing her embarrassing blunder.

"Elder Yao, I'm sorry that I released my Yang Qi all over your face." Su Yang apologized to her.

"It's fine—"

Elder Yao halted her words midway when she noticed that Su Yang's rod was still as hard as a rock. She had heard from others that men typically go limp for hours after ejaculating once.

'Dual cultivators are truly a different breed…'

Elder Yao stood up and said, "I'm going to wash my face."

"What about the Yang Qi? We can still use it, right?" Su Yang asked.

"No, it cannot have any contaminations, so we can no longer use it." "I see… then I'll just have to release some more. Do you mind helping me again, Elder Yao?"

"..."

"I'm not going to let my effort be in vain, so I need to make sure you give your submission today!" she said as she stormed to the bathroom.

However, once she was inside the bathroom, Elder Yao didn't immediately wash the Yang Qi away.

Instead, she used her finger to scoop some off her face before examining it.

"As I thought, his Yang Qi has improved tremendously in quality since the last time I saw him. Did he consume a priceless treasure or something? No wonder why he dares to participate in the Yang Qi King!" she muttered.

"But it still cannot be compared to his friend's Yang Qi…"

While lost in her thoughts, Elder Yao subconsciously moved her finger to her mouth and licked it.

"?!"

Elder Yao's eyes widened with shock when she realized what she'd just done.

'Did I just willingly taste his Yang Qi?! There's something wrong with me today!'

Elder Yao swiftly splashed cold water all over her face and began washing the Yang Qi away.

A few minutes later, Elder Yao came out of the bathroom.

"Alright, let's do this again. If you dare release your Yang Qi before I am prepared again, I'll kill you," she said to him as she kneeled before his rod and loosened the upper half of her robes, revealing her perky breasts again.

"I wouldn't dare." Su Yang said with a smile.

"..."

Elder Yao began stroking Su Yang's sword again, and even though she'd just cleared her mind, it didn't take long for her to fall into a daze again.

The desire to put Su Yang's meat stick in her mouth grew stronger and stronger, but she constantly reminded herself that she'd contaminate his Yang Qi with her saliva, which helped control her self-restraint.

Sometime later, Su Yang said, "Elder Yao, I think I might need something more stimulating for a second release…"

Elder Yao glared at him with a deep frown on her face, but she didn't say anything.

After a moment of silence, Elder Yao withdrew her hands from Su Yang's sword.

Then, she moved forward and leaned her chest closer to Su Yang until his rod was firmly pressed between her perky breasts.

She began moving a moment later.

"How's this?" she asked after giving him a taste.

"It's great and very stimulating." Su Yang said with a satisfied smile.

Although Elder Yao lacked technique, her sexiness more than made up for it.

However, Elder Yao quickly regretted giving him a boob job because it brought Su Yang's sword much closer to her face, enticing her even further.

When she could no longer endure it any longer, she hastily said, "If you can release your Yang Qi within the next three minutes, I'll give you a reward."

"What kind of reward?"

"I'll clean you up with my mouth," she said in a bashful voice.

Su Yang's eyes flickered with excitement.

"I'll do my best!"

chapters.title: Chapter 1095 Elder Yao’S Reward
chapters.body:
Chapter 1095 Elder Yao’S Reward

Chapter 1095 Elder Yao's Reward

Despite Elder Yao's enticing offer, Su Yang didn't immediately release his Yang Qi. Doing so would reveal that he had been capable of releasing it at any time—that he'd been messing with her this entire time.

Chapter 1096 Because I Am Here!

Chapter 1096 Because I Am Here!

Upon entering Su Yang's home, Lin Xinyi took a seat at the table in the living room.

"So, you want to know why you're at the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, correct?"

"From what you've told me, I believe you want to understand love."

"Love…?" Lin Xinyi's eyes widened, seemingly in disbelief at its simplicity.

Su Yang narrowed his eyes at her and said, "You're probably thinking there's no way it's something as simple as love, right?"

"Well, you're wrong. While love may seem simple on the surface, it's actually a very complicated thing. There are many different types of love."

"However, I don't think you're at the Boundless Yin Yang Sect only because you want to understand love. You are also curious. You want to understand the relationships between the God of Pleasure and his family. Unfortunately, you will not find the answer you seek here."

"This is especially true if you want to understand the feeling of love. While I can't say that there is no love to be found in this sort of environment, it's very different from the rest of the world."

"The disciples here love pleasure. I wouldn't be surprised if more than half of the disciples in the sect have no feelings for each other—that they only cultivate for the pleasure and their own benefit."

"Of course, I am not saying that's a bad thing. That's just the nature of dual cultivators. It's much harder for us to experience genuine love, but when it does happen, it's the greatest feeling in the world." Su Yang spoke with a gentle smile on his face, as if he was speaking from experience.

Lin Xinyi stared at Su Yang's face with a somewhat dazed expression. Did she really come to the Boundless Yin Yang Sect to understand love? Why does she even want to understand it in the first place?

After a moment of silence, Lin Xinyi spoke in a low voice, "My father started paying less attention to my mother after getting a second wife. Things only got worse from there as he acquired more wives. My mother constantly worries that he'll eventually completely ignore her or, worse, kick her out of the family like trash."

"Alas, my mother's worries came true after my father acquired his seventh wife a few years ago."

"Although he didn't kick her out of the family, he stopped paying attention to her, and it made my mother feel invisible, a feeling worse than death to her. No matter how much my mother tried to regain his attention, her attempts only made things worse and angered the other wives, who were also fighting for the same attention. Eventually, they started working together to get rid of my mother."

"In the end, my mother fell sick from stress and died. After my mother's death, I left the family and joined the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, a place that was said to be the birth of the God of Pleasure."

"I not only admire the God of Pleasure, but I also envy his wives. If my mother had been one of them, she would definitely still be alive right now."

Tears flowed from Lin Xinyi's eyes as she revealed her story to Su Yang.

"..."

This wasn't Su Yang's first hearing of such a story. In his previous life, many of his partners were mistresses who had been mistreated, just like Lin Xinyi's mother.

Although it wasn't true love, Su Yang showed them the love they should've gotten from their worthless husbands.

However, when said husbands found out their wives had sought love from someone else, they immediately became enraged and blamed Su Yang for everything, ignoring the reason it happened in the first place.

"Do you think I made a mistake by coming here…?" Lin Xinyi suddenly asked after wiping her tears.

"Even if you did make a mistake, it's okay. The most important thing is that you realize and acknowledge your mistake. With that said, I don't think you made any mistakes. If anything, you made the right choice by coming here. Do you know why?"

"Why?"

Su Yang smiled and said, "Because I am here! If I weren't here, it would've been a mistake, but luckily for you, that's not the case!"

"What kind of nonsense is that?"

Despite Su Yang's narcissistic words, Lin Xinyi cracked a chuckle.

"Are you trying to tell me that you'll teach me love?" she then asked.

"You want to know how it feels to be one of the God of Pleasure's wives, right? I can make it a reality."

"How will you do that?" As ridiculous as it sounded, Su Yang had piqued Lin Xinyi's interest.

"I will make you my partner in the Boundless Yin Yang Sect, but you won't be the only one. I will have several other partners besides you. However, I promise that you'll all receive the same amount of 'love' from me. It might not be completely the same, but it's close. What do you think?"

"You can't be serious…" Lin Xinyi stared at him with a bewildered face.

"Do I look like I am joking?" Su Yang spoke with a serious expression.

"That's the most outrageous thing I've heard in my life!"

"Is it really? Did you forget that we're in the Boundless Yin Yang Sect? As dual cultivators, it is completely normal for us to have more than one partner."

"..." Lin Xinyi fell silent, as she could not argue with his logic.

"Of course, I can't force you to become my partner. However, if you agree to be my partner, I can guarantee you'll be envied by every disciple in this sect, and you'll know how it feels to be a wife to the God of Pleasure."

After a moment of silence, Lin Xinyi nodded with a flushed face, "Okay, I'll be your partner."

Sometime later, they arrived at the Pavilion of Declaration, situated on a small, remote floating island. In addition to the pavilion, the island featured a serene pond and several tall stone tablets, each engraved with thousands of names.

The stone tablets recorded every official partnership in the sect, from Outer Court disciples to Core Disciples. Not many disciples were present when they arrived, but that was to be expected. "That's Xiao Yang!" However, the Outer Court disciples there immediately recognized him.

All eyes were on Su Yang and Lin Xinyi as they approached the Pavilion of Declaration. The building itself was small and simple, with just a single floor. Inside, a Sect Elder sat cultivating behind a desk at the room's center while several doors stood closed behind him.

The Sect Elder, a young man, opened his eyes when he sensed Su Yang and Lin Xinyi's presence approaching his desk.

"Are you here to declare your partnership or to break one?" he inquired.

"We're here to declare our relationship."

"Introduce yourselves."

"Xiao Yang, Outer Court disciple."

"Lin Xinyi, Outer Court disciple."

The Sect Elder then pointed at the first room to their left and said, "Go to room one."

Su Yang and Lin Xinyi didn't question it and entered the room.

However, Lin Xinyi raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner when she saw the interior of the room.

"Why is there a bed and shower here?" she mused out loud.

"You don't know? We have to cultivate here to prove that we're really partners to prevent fake partnerships," Su Yang explained.

"Why would someone want to fake their partnership?" she questioned innocently.

"Some do it to avoid being expelled from the sect for being unable to find partners, while others do it to keep others away."

"I see… that makes sense…"

"Then shall we get started?" Su Yang suddenly started taking off his clothes.

She sighed, "If I had known we needed to cultivate here, I would've waited a few more days before coming."

Although she'd recovered from their recent cultivation session, it was only to the point where she could walk.

"Don't worry, I'll go easy."

Lin Xinyi removed her clothes and entered the bed with Su Yang a moment later.

"Mmmh! Ahhn~! Harder~!"

Although their cultivation session began tame and gentle, it didn't take long for things to intensify. As they got deeper into the mood, Lin Xinyi started begging for more, her voice filled with eagerness and desire.

In the end, they cultivated for two hours straight, somehow beating their previous session.

Afterward, Lin Xinyin was so exhausted that she couldn't even lift a finger, so Su Yang gave her a pill that sped up her recovery.

While Su Yang and Lin Xinyi were inside the room, news of their partnership spread throughout the Outer Court like wildfire.

"Did you hear? Fairy Lin ended up picking Disciple Xiao as her first partner!" "No way. You're messing with me."

"Don't believe me? You can see for yourself at the Pavilion of Declaration!"

Most didn't believe this rumor, so they traveled to the Pavilion of Declaration to see for themselves.

When Su Yang and Lin Xinyi emerged from their room two and a half hours later, they were surprised to find the area swarming with Outer Court disciples.

"Alright, you're both recognized as partners now." The Sect Elder announced after writing their names in a book.

Shortly later, their names appeared on one of the stone tablets.

"Good heavens! It's true! Fairy Lin has accepted Disciple Xiao as her partner!"

"Disciple Lin, that lucky girl! Now she can experience Disciple Xiao's godly techniques as much as she wants!"

"Noooo! Not my fairy!"

A commotion stirred with many cries of despair resounded. freewёbn૦νeɭ.com

"One of the Unconquerable Faries has finally been conquered! When and who will conquer Fairy Fei?!"

Once Su Yang and Lin Xinyi exited the building, they were warmed and surrounded by disciples.

"Why did you pick Disciple Xiao as your partner? Is it because of his techniques?"

"Disciple Xiao, I am willing to be your partner, too! Please make me your partner!"

As Lin Xinyi started feeling overwhelmed, Su Yang suddenly held her by the waist and flew away, ignoring the rowdy crowd.

The crowd tried to chase after them, but Su Yang swiftly left them behind with his superior movement technique.

"I didn't think our partnership would cause such a commotion…" Lin Xinyi sighed as they returned to the Outcast Peak.

"It'll quiet down soon," said Su Yang.

"Anyway, now that we're partners, do you want to move in to my living quarters? It'll be more convenient for our cultivation sessions."

She nodded, "But give me a few days… Too much has happened today, and I need some time to myself for now."

"Take your time." "Thank you. Then I'll see you later."

Lin Xinyi returned to her living quarters shortly after.

chapters.title: Chapter 1100 Commotion Outside Lin Xinyi’S Residence
chapters.body:
Chapter 1100 Commotion Outside Lin Xinyi’S Residence

Chapter 1100 Commotion Outside Lin Xinyi’s Residence

News of Su Yang and Lin Xinyi's official partnership continued to spread throughout the sect even long after they left the Pavilion of Declaration.

When the disciples who had attempted to court Lin Xinyi but failed heard the news, they were understandably upset. Some, especially the Inner Court and Core Disciples, took the news harder than others.

"I lost to some fucking Outer Court disciple who lives in the Outcast Peak?! Impossible!" An Inner Court disciple punched a hole in their wall after learning of this news.

"She dares to choose an Outer Court disciple over me, a Core Disciple?! Where is that logic in that?! From what I heard, that Xiao Yang has an ordinary appearance, too!"

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Outer Court.

"Sister Fei, did you hear? That Lin Xinyi actually accepted a partner before you did!"

"Oh, really? Good for her." Disciple Fei mumbled in a disinterested voice.

"You know, I actually made a bet with Sister Mu—a bet to see who will acquire a partner first—you or Lin Xinyi. I bet you'd find a partner first." Her roommate said.

"Why'd you bet on me?" she asked.

"You seem more serious about finding a partner, and you're much more popular than her."

"Serious about finding a partner, huh?" Disciple Fei muttered with a seemingly distant gaze.

"By the way, you'll never believe who she chose as her partner. Do you know that disciple who recently joined the sect and started a huge commotion in the Outer Court?" her roommate continued.

"That's right! It's Disciple Xiao! That massager at the Outcast Peak!"

Disciple Fei's eyebrows twitched after being reminded of him.freeweɓnovēl.coɱ

"It's him?"

Disciple Fei wore a surprised expression on her beautiful face.

"Oh? What's this? You're actually interested?" her roommate showed a teasing smile, as Disciple Fei rarely showed interest in anything.

"Don't tell me you're also interested in Disciple Xiao?"

"No… that's not it." Disciple Fei shook her head.

"To be honest, I am kind of envious of Lin Xinyi. I have experienced Disciple Xiao's massage, and it was godly. He definitely knows how to pleasure a woman, and I can only imagine how good it'll feel to cultivate with him."

Disciple Fei looked at her roommate with a dumbfounded face.

"Are you looking down on me? You'd understand me if you'd experienced his massage, too."

"That will never happen." Disciple Fei sneered.

"Anyway, I am going to cultivate."

Disciple Fei returned to her room the next moment.

"Disciple Xiao…" she mumbled with a pondering face after closing the door.

A few days later, talks about Su Yang and Lin Xinyi slowed, as every disciple in the Outer Court had heard of it by now.

During these few days, numerous male disciples visited Lin Xinyi's residence, each attempting to persuade her to reconsider her partnership with Su Yang.

"Fairy Lin! Please reconsider! You'll definitely be happier with me!"

"Nothing good will come out of partnering with that troublemaker!"

Eventually, the door opened, but it was not Lin Xinyi who came out.

"Can you all just leave already?! It's obvious that Disciple Lin doesn't want to listen to any of you!" Her roommate, Disciple Ru, shouted at the crowd outside their home.

"We're only here to talk to Fairy Lin, so stop sticking your nose into our business!"

"That's right! If you want us to leave, let us speak with Fairy Lin!"

Lin Xinyi's roommate sneered and spoke as she glared at them with disdain, "What a bunch of sore losers! You were unable to court Disciple Lin before, so what makes you think you'll change her mind now? Get lost before I call for an Elder!"

"What did you say, you bitch?!"

"Who the hell are you, anyway?!"

Suddenly, a figure approached them. This individual was a tall and handsome man, and every step he took exuded immense grace.

"It's a Core Disciple!" The disciples there exclaimed after recognizing his disciple outfit.

"That's Senior apprentice-brother Yun Xiao Hong! He was also trying to court Fairy Lin but failed!"

"What brings you here, Senior Apprentice-brother Yun?" Lin Xinyi's roommate greeted him with a respectful bow.

"I came here to ask Junior Sister Lin a question. Can you let her know that I am here?" Yun Xiao Hong responded with a calm smile on his face.

"Okay… I'll let her know." Her roommate nodded, reluctantly agreeing to his request. Due to the difference in their disciple status, she had no choice but to treat him respectfully and listen to his request as long as it wasn't unreasonable.

"Sister Lin, Core Disciple Yun Xiao Hong is here to see you… I can deal with the Outer Court disciples, but he's different. I'm sorry, but I cannot afford to offend a Core Disciple." Lin Xinyi came out of her room with an apologetic smile and said, "Thank you for everything, Sister Qin. I'll speak with the Core Disciple."

Although she could easily ignore the Outer Court disciples crying outside her home, a Core Disciple was an entirely different matter.

A few moments later, Lin Xinyi went outside to confront Yun Xiao Hong.

"This Junior greets Senior Apprentice-brother Yun. How can I assist you today?"

"Disciple Lin, when you refused to be my cultivation partner, I thought it was because I was lacking, so I wasn't too upset and accepted the results. However, you accepted an Outer Court disciple who lives in the Outcast Peak as your partner, and I cannot fathom why. I doubt there's anything an Outer Court disciple has that I, a Core Disciple, lack," Yun Xiao Hong stated confidently.

"So, can you answer this question for me? Why did you choose that Outer Court disciple over me, who is clearly superior in every way?"

"..."

The entire place fell silent, and everyone there turned to look at Lin Xinyi, who had a perplexed expression on her face.

There was simply no way for her to answer that question without offending the Core Disciple.

"I will not leave until I receive an answer, Disciple Lin," Yun Xiao Hong pressured.

Lin Xinyi clenched her fists, and just as her lips parted, a clear voice resounded, "I can answer that question for you."

"Who the—?"

Everyone turned their heads to see an ordinary-looking young man approaching them with confident strides.

Their eyes widened with shock after realizing his identity.

"Xiao Yang?" Lin Xinyi muttered his name with a dazed face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1101 Provoking A Core Disciple
chapters.body:
Chapter 1101 Provoking A Core Disciple

Chapter 1101 Provoking a Core Disciple

1101 Provoking a Core Disciple

"Xiao Yang? So you're Disciple Xiao." Yun Xiao Hong frowned deeply after seeing Su Yang's ordinary face.

He spat on the ground and sneered in disgust, "I was told you were ugly, but I could've never imagined it would be to this extent. How did you even get into the sect with that face?"

After seeing Su Yang's ordinary face with his own eyes, Yun Xiao Hong became even more enraged that Lin Xinyi had chosen Su Yang over him.

"This is unacceptable, Disciple Lin! I cannot accept this! Are you telling me that he's better than me in your eyes?!" Yun Xiao Hong turned to her and roared.

"Hey, why are you barking outside my partner's residence? Even a dog has more manners than you," Su Yang suddenly mocked.

The disciples there looked at Su Yang with terrified expressions on their faces.

'Did he just mock a Core Disciple, calling him worse than a dog?! He's fucking dead!'

'Hahaha! It looks like Fairy Lin will become single again in no time!'

Lin Xinyi covered her mouth from shock, her eyes filled with worry.

Yun Xiao Hong's face turned red after realizing he'd just been mocked.

However, before he could speak, Su Yang continued, "Anyway, let me answer your question. She chose me because I am superior. It's as simple as that."

"WATCH YOUR MOUTH!"

Yun Xiao Hong suddenly used a movement technique and lunged his fist at Su Yang's face. However, he had no intention of trying to kill Su Yang. He only wanted to rough up Su Yang's face until it was unrecognizable.

Unauthorized fighting amongst disciples was prohibited, but as a Core Disciple, Yun Xiao Hong had some leeway.

Right before the fist landed on his face, Su Yang caught it, even doing so with seemingly no effort.

'What?! He caught my fist?!' Yun Xiao Hong exclaimed inwardly.

Yun Xiao Hong was at the ninth level of the Divine Origin Realm, and Su Yang appeared to be at the first level of the Divine Spirit Realm because he suppressed his cultivation.

"Launching a sneak attack against an Outer Court disciple while being a Core Disciple? You should be ashamed of yourself." Su Yang shook his head.

"Let go of my hand!" Yun Xiao Hong roared as he swung his arm.

Su Yang swiftly released his hand and proceeded to wipe it with his robes.

"You think I want to touch you? Disgusting." Su Yang shrugged.

"You're going overboard, Disciple Xiao! How can you disrespect a Core Disciple like this?!" The Outer Court disciples suddenly started yelling at him when they saw a chance to impress the Core Disciple.

"That's right! You're going too far! Apologize to him immediately!"

However, Su Yang started laughing and said, "So what if he's a Core Disciple? There's nothing special about it."

"How dare you look down on Core Disciples, you ignorant bastard who just joined the sect!" Yun Xiao Hong shouted with veins on his face.

"Senior apprentice-brother Yun is right! You're just an Outer Court disciple yourself! What do you know about being a Core Disciple?!"

"I would love to see you become one yourself!"

The other disciples supported Yun Xiao Hong.

Su Yang smiled and said, "You want to see me become a Core Disciple? Very well, I'll become one within three months."

The disciples were baffled by his bold declaration.

"You'll become a Core Disciple within three months?! Hahaha!" Yun Xiao Hong burst out laughing.

The others laughed with him.

A few moments later, Yun Xiao Hong abruptly stopped laughing and glared at Su Yang, "What an unfunny joke. Let alone three months, you won't become a Core Disciple even if I were to give you thirty years!"

Su Yang's calm expression didn't change as he responded, "If I cannot become a Core Disciple within three months, I'll leave this sect myself."

"Xiao Yang?! What are you saying?! Quickly, take that back!" Lin Xinyi cried out loud.

He looked at her with a confident smile and said, "I know what I am saying. Trust me."

"..."

Despite his outrageous claims, Lin Xinyi found herself feeling an inexplicable sense of trust toward him.

'How come I don't feel doubtful even in the slightest? Is it because of his smile?' she wondered inwardly.

"You said it yourself, Disciple Xiao. If you cannot become a Core Disciple within three months, you'll leave this sect! And once you're no longer a disciple, I'll fucking torture you myself!" Yun Xiao Hong said.

Su Yang nodded, "But if I manage to become a Core Disciple… the first thing I'll do is have a deathmatch with you."

"Haha! Of course! I won't refuse! I swear on it!" Yun Xiao Hong declared.

Yun Xiao Hong turned around and walked away shortly after.

Su Yang glared at the Outer Court disciples afterward and said, "What are you lot still doing here? Are you looking to enter the arena with me?"

They instantly recalled Su Yang's life-or-death match with Shao Jianjun and trembled in fear.

Without uttering a word, they quickly scrambled away.

Lin Xinyi rushed over to Su Yang once they were alone, tears flowing down her face.

"The other didn't matter, but you shouldn't have provoked that Core Disciple, Xiao Yang!"

Su Yang rubbed her back and said, "Calm down. Even if he's a Core Disciple, he's only a disciple at the end of the day. The God of Pleasure would offend even the Heavenly Emperor for his wives."

"But you're not the God of Pleasure! I appreciate what you're trying to do, but I don't want you getting hurt for it! And you just did the worst thing a disciple can do in the sect—offend a Core Disciple!"

Su Yang laughed, "So what? I'll also become a Core Disciple in three months, anyway."

"That's…" Lin Xinyi fell silent.

"Once I win the Yang Qi King, I'll immediately become an Inner Court disciple. Then, I'll become a Core Disciple within two months." Su Yang revealed his plans to her, leaving her speechless.

"You're participating in the Yang Qi King…?" she muttered in a daze.

chapters.title: Chapter 1102: In Three Weeks
chapters.body:
Chapter 1102: In Three Weeks

Chapter 1102: In Three Weeks

"That's right. Surprised?" Su Yang chuckled after seeing Lin Xinyi's dazed face.

"A little. I've heard that Outer Court disciples rarely participate because Inner Court and Core Disciples can also participate, and the winner is always a Core Disciple."

"Core Disciples have more time and resources to cultivate their Yang Qi, after all. It's essentially impossible for an Outer Court disciple to win."

"If you know that, then why…" Lin Xinyi mumbled.

"Have you forgotten already? I am going to be a Core Disciple within three months, and this is just a stepping stone to help me achieve that goal. If it weren't for the sect rules, I could easily become a Sect Elder with my qualifications, much less a Core Disciple."

Su Yang spoke with such confidence that Lin Xinyi couldn't help but believe him.

"Anyway, are you ready to move to my place? I came here to check on you after hearing that you've been hounded by disciples lately."

Lin Xinyi nodded, "I had been prepared to leave since yesterday, but with those disciples surrounding my place, I didn't dare to go outside."

Su Yang chuckled, "What are you afraid of? That they'd bite you? They may act like stray dogs, but these types of people are typically just cowards who only know how to bark. That's why you don't need to be afraid of them."

"Come, I'll bring you to my place."

"Give me a few moments."

Lin Xinyi went back inside to thank her roommate for everything she'd done before leaving with Su Yang.

Once they returned to the Outcast Peak, since there were only three bedrooms, Lin Xinyi moved into Mei Xing's room while Mei Xing shared a room with her mother.

'Why do I instantly feel so much safer once I enter his home?' Lin Xinyi wondered inwardly.

She thought it was due to Su Yang's presence, but in reality, it was mostly because of the hidden formations that surrounded the house.

"Now then, let's cultivate, shall we?" Su Yang said with a smile.

"Already?" Lin Xinyi didn't think they'd start so soon.

"Of course. If you didn't know, most Outer Court disciples cultivate at least six days a week."

"That much?" Lin Xinyi truly didn't know the disciples cultivated so much.

"How do you pass your days as a disciple without a partner?" Su Yang asked. He was actually curious since he never spent more than a few days without partners when he was a disciple before.

"I cultivate normally and study the sect's techniques to prepare for when I do have a partner," she said.

"I see. But no matter how much you cultivate normally, you'll never catch up with the disciples who dual cultivate often. This is why your cultivation is so much weaker than the others."

Lin Xinyi was only at the fourth level of the Sovereign Spirit Realm. Compared to the average Outer Court disciples, who were typically between the seventh and ninth level of the Sovereign Spirit Realm, she was quite lacking.

Lin Xinyi sighed. As much as she hated to hear it, she couldn't refute it. Due to being single for over a year, she was drastically behind the other Outer Court disciples.

Seeing her reaction, Su Yang smiled and said, "Even if you're ten—a hundred years behind, you will catch up and surpass them now that you are my partner."

Lin Xinyi raised her eyebrows in a doubtful manner.

"Don't believe me? I will make you breakthrough the Sovereign Spirit Realm before the Yang Qi King result is announced."

Lin Xinyi's eyes widened at his bold claims.

"From the fourth level Sovereign Spirit Realm to the Divine Spirit Realm in three weeks?! That's impossible, no matter how I think about it!"

"It's not going to be easy, but as long as you follow my instructions and try your best not to give up, I promise you'll reach the Divine Spirit Realm."

Lin Xinyin wore a dazed expression on her face for a brief moment before she nodded with a resolute face.

"Good. Then strip. We'll begin now."

Lin Xinyi entered his room and removed her clothes, revealing her slender body before entering the bed.

Su Yang also stripped and followed her to the bed.

After caressing her smooth white skin and playing with her humble breasts for some time, Su Yang inserted his rod inside her body and began their dual cultivation session.

Lin Xinyi lasted only an hour at most during their last session. Yet, their current session lasted for ten hours straight.freё.com

"I think I am dying…" Lin Xinyi muttered with an incredibly vulgar expression on her face. Ten hours of immense pleasure was almost too much for someone who'd only recently experienced her first time.

"Start refining my Yang Qi, and you'll feel better," Su Yang said.

Lin Xinyi closed her eyes and began reciting the Boundless Yin Yang Sect's dual cultivation technique inside her head, quickly refining the abundant Yang Qi inside her body.

'Why does his Yang Qi contain so much Profound Qi? Even consuming a Golden Yin Pill can't compare to a single drop of his Yang Qi!' Lin Xinyi thought to herself, quickly becoming mesmerized by the overwhelming sensation of absorbing so much Profound Qi that it felt like she was swimming in an ocean of it.

Four hours later, Lin Xinyi opened her eyes.

"Ready for the second session?" Su Yang's voice resounded right beside her ear, startling her.

Although she was still tired, Lin Xinyi could feel her cultivation already nearing the next level, encouraging her to continue.

'I had a breakthrough just a few days ago, and I am already about to breakthrough again… If I continue at this rate, reaching the Divine Spirit Realm before the Yang Qi King concludes is possible!' Lin Xinyi looked at Su Yang with determination and excitement.

"I'll be in your care," she said.

Su Yang gave her a passionate kiss on the lips before sticking his rod inside her already soaking-wet cave, beginning their second cultivation session.

chapters.title: Chapter 1103: Laughingstock
chapters.body:
Chapter 1103: Laughingstock

Chapter 1103: Laughingstock

Su Yang and Lin Xinyi cultivated with each other almost nonstop for three weeks straight, secluding themselves inside Su Yang's room the entire time.

While they cultivated, news of Su Yang's bold claims to become a Core Disciple within three months spread like wildfire throughout the entire sect, making Su Yang a laughingstock and the center of mockery.

Naturally, Su Yang's death match with Yun Xiao Hong also spread at the same time. His blatant disrespect toward a Core Disciple and his belittling of their status as a whole instantly made almost every Core Disciple in the sect hostile toward him. Some even threatened to kill him if they were met.

This commotion caught the attention of the sect elders, but there was nothing they could do.

"Master, have you heard about the recent news on Xiao Yang?" Luo Li approached her master, Elder Chu, after hearing the news herself.

"Who hasn't heard of it? That fool not only picked a fight with a Core Disciple but also offended almost every Core Disciple with his bold claims! That idiot! I told him to stay lowkey!" Elder Chu cursed out loud.

"What should we do about it? The Sect Leader said we must protect him at all costs, right?"

"The Sect Leader is still away, and unless Disciple Xiao's life is endangered, we have no reason to bother her. We'll just keep a closer eye on him for now."

"What if the Core Disciples decide to act?" Luo Li continued to ask, clearly worried about his well-being.

"What do you know about our Core Disciples? They can make all the threats they want, but they won't act against a mere Outer Court Disciple. However, things will change once he becomes a Core Disciple—even an Inner Court disciple. If and when that happens, they'll likely start troubling him. However, the Sect Leader should have returned by that time."

"So, in three months?" Luo Li said.

"As if! There's no way he could become a Core Disciple in three months. It's simply impossible. Even if he meets the cultivation standard, he won't have enough contribution points to qualify." Elder Chu was confident that Su Yang would not be able to accomplish his claims, sharing the same sentiment as the rest of the sect.

Meanwhile, Elder Yao rubbed her eyes in the corner, looking like she hadn't slept in a while.

"Are you okay, Elder Yao? You don't look good." Elder Chu said to her.freē.com

"I'm fine. I'm just feeling a little stressed because of that idiot. If anything happens to him, my pills will take a nosedive in quality…" she sighed.

'And that bastard got a partner right after that day… Does it really mean nothing to him?' Elder Yao cried inwardly as she clenched her fists in frustration. She hadn't been able to focus on her alchemy after giving special assistance to Su Yang. Yet, Su Yang managed to get a partner.

"I'm definitely going to give him a beating the next time we meet!" she muttered.

"By the way, Elder Yao, you came here because you wanted to tell me something, right? What is it?" Elder Chu suddenly inquired.

They were in the middle of a conversation when Luo Li interrupted.

"Oh, I was just going to let you know that Xiao Yang will participate in this year's Yang Qi King. He's already submitted his Yang Qi, too."

"What?!" Luo Li was greatly surprised to hear this.

"Why would he do such a thing? His Yang Qi is nowhere near what it takes to win," Elder Chu pondered out loud.

"About that… His Yang Qi had a sudden breakthrough. It's to the point where it almost rivals the Yang Qi of his friend," she revealed.

"What?! Impossible! He must've cheated using his friend's Yang Qi!"

"No… that's not possible. I watched him submit his Yang Qi." Elder Yao shook her head.

"You watched him? So he had you submit it?" Elder Chu asked.

Elder Yao silently nodded, refusing to elaborate or give any more details.

"Does that mean he actually has the ability to control the quality of his Yang Qi, just like the Sect Leader suspected? Or did he have a fortuitous encounter in the last few months?" Elder Chu wondered out loud.

"I believe it's the latter, Master." Luo Li said. "He's been going out and doing high-priority missions recently, so it's likely he had a fortuitous encounter at that time."

"I see… but to increase the quality of his Yang Qi so much in such a short time… I cannot imagine what kind of heaven-defying treasure he'd managed to stumble upon…" Elder Chu sighed, her voice with a hint of envy.

"Do you think he has a chance of winning, Elder Yao?" Luo Li asked, her eyes filled with intrigue.

"Hard to say, but it's not impossible…" she said.

"I hope he doesn't think he can earn enough contribution from winning the Yang Qi King to become a Core Disciple…" Elder Chu sighed.

"I guess we'll see what he does in the next three months." Elder Yao said.

Time passed, and in the blink of an eye, it was already time for the result of this year's Yang Qi King to be announced.

The results will be revealed at the floating island where the matchmaking event took place.

"Who do you think will become this year's Yang Qi King?" The disciples spoke to each other while the names of the participants began appearing on a massive jade tablet.

"Definitely a Core Disciple."

"Hey, look at the name that just appeared! Xiao Yang! He actually participated in the Yang Qi King, too!"

The name Xiao Yang quickly attracted everyone's attention for a brief moment as they mocked it.

"Xiao Yang? That frog in a well who claims he will become a Core Disciple in three months? How laughable."

"If he becomes this year's Yang Qi King, I'll quit being a dual cultivator!" An Inner Court disciple shouted with a smug look on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1104 Yang Qi King’S Result
chapters.body:
Chapter 1104 Yang Qi King’S Result

Chapter 1104 Yang Qi King's Result

"You'll quit being a dual cultivator if I become the Yang Qi King? That's a bold statement. Are you willing to swear a heaven's oath, or are you just barking?" A calm voice suddenly resounded, calling out the Inner Court disciple who'd just spoken.

Everyone there turned to look at the source of the voice and saw Su Yang casually approaching them with a beauty walking by his side.

"It's Disciple Xiao and Fairy Lin!"

"Is it just me, or is there something very different about Fairy Lin? She used to have this reserved demeanor, but now her aura is oozing with confidence."

"That's Fairy Lin?! I didn't recognize her until you mentioned it!"

The Outer Court disciples instantly noticed the drastic change in Lin Xinyi's demeanor. The once shy and timid girl had disappeared, becoming a confident, self-assured woman.

Suddenly, one of the female disciples there shouted in disbelief, "What the?! How is she at the second level of the Divine Spirit Realm?! She was only at the third level of the Sovereign Spirit Realm just a month ago!"

Once this was pointed out, others began to realize that it wasn't just her demeanor that had changed. Her cultivation had also improved at a drastic, seemingly impossible speed, leaving everyone astonished and puzzled.

Lin Xinyi's face blushed slightly after becoming the center of attention.

'I thought I was prepared for this, but reality is often disappointing. I still have a lot of work to do,' she sighed inwardly.

"Are you telling me that she had seven breakthroughs in just a month? How is that possible?" The Inner Court disciples did not believe the Outer Court disciples, and their doubt was reasonable.

Su Yang stopped walking once he stood before a certain Inner Court disciple.
