For a Core Disciple like him, such a remark was nothing short of humiliation.

"In the Limitless Sword Sect, one must at least comprehend Basic Sword Will to become a Core Disciple!"

"Then let’s have a brief exchange using Basic Sword Will," Su Yang then said.

"Right now? And here?" The man asked.

He nodded.

"It’ll only be a single exchange. Are you scared?"

"Of course I’m worried—worried that I might accidentally kill you!" he shouted angrily. "I’m here as a guest. Even as a Core Disciple of the Limitless Sword Sect, I cannot kill a disciple from another sect on their own territory! You might as well ask me to commit suicide!"

"Don’t worry, I promise nothing will happen to you even if you accidentally kill me. I swear." Su Yang said.

He then turned to look at Lin Xinyi and continued, "You’ll be the witness. If anything happens to me, let the others know what I said, okay?"

"Are you sure this is a good idea...?" Lin Xinyi asked with a worried frown.

They were just about to head to the Eternal Yin Yang Caves, so how had they suddenly ended up in such a dangerous situation?

Although Su Yang had defeated Yun Xiao Hong, his current opponent was a Core Disciple of the Limitless Sword Sect, a powerful sect renowned for its combat prowess. Compared to such a figure, Yun Xiao Hong was simply not on the same level.

However, she decided to trust Su Yang.

"Okay, I swear I will let the Elders know that he won’t need to take responsibility if anything happens to you," she said a moment later.

Su Yang returned to the man and smiled.

"You heard her. Or do you have another excuse to avoid embarrassing yourself?"

The handsome man trembled at Su Yang’s words. Without saying another word, he retrieved a beautiful green sword from his spatial ring.

Raising the sword before him, he declared, "My name is Wang Haoran, and I accept your challenge."

"I didn’t think I’d get to use this sword so soon, but..."

Su Yang retrieved his newly acquired Empyrean-grade sword and introduced himself, "My name is Xiao Yang."

The two of them remained motionless like statues for nearly a full minute. Then, without warning, they both swung their swords at the exact same moment.

In the next instant, their Sword Wills collided.

"Impossible!" Wang Haoran exclaimed in shock before abruptly twisting his body, as though trying to evade something unseen.

"Cough!"

The next second, Wang Haoran coughed up a mouthful of blood. Although he had managed to avoid the Sword Will itself, the aura it released, merely brushing past him, was enough to injure his internal organs.

He hurriedly covered his mouth before turning to glare at Su Yang, his eyes wide with shock and disbelief.

Su Yang calmly met his gaze and said in a disappointed tone, "Was that your best shot? You had my hopes up for nothing. I thought the Limitless Sword Sect had grown much stronger since it managed to defeat the Celestial Sword Sect. If this is your level, I can’t imagine how you managed to defeat them."

"Y-You... who the hell are you?!" Wang Haoran shouted, his gaze trembling with bewilderment and fear.

chapters.title: Chapter 1144: Arriving At The Eternal Yin Yang Cave
chapters.body:
Chapter 1144: Arriving At The Eternal Yin Yang Cave

Chapter 1144: Arriving at the Eternal Yin Yang Cave

"I am merely an ordinary disciple of the Boundless Yin Yang Sect," Su Yang replied calmly.

"Bullshit! There’s no way a disciple from a place like this could wield Sword Will!" Wang Haoran refused to accept that his Sword Will was inferior to that of a dual cultivator. If his fellow disciples were to hear of this humiliation, he would become the laughingstock of the sect for the rest of his life.

"No way?" Su Yang shook his head with a sigh. "Yet you just experienced it yourself."

"T-That’s not what I meant! I bet you’re not really a disciple here at all! You must be a guest like me, just pretending to be one!"

"If you have any doubts about my identity, you may ask around. Anyway, now that I have proven myself, I’d like to take my leave. We’ve already delayed our business to entertain you long enough."

Su Yang turned around and started flying away after putting away his sword. Lin Xinyi silently followed.

"Wait! We’re not done—"

"No, we’re done." Su Yang suddenly turned and glared at Wang Haoran, a dangerous glint flickering in his eyes.

Wang Haoran froze the moment he sensed Su Yang’s killing intent. His instincts screamed that if he dared utter another word or move even a muscle, Su Yang would kill him on the spot.

Su Yang continued leaving—only to stop again.

When Wang Haoran saw this, he subconsciously closed his eyes, accepting his death.

"Oh, right." Su Yang said out loud. "If you ever get the chance, tell Jiang Wushuang to wash his neck and wait for his retribution."

’Jiang... Wushuang?’ Wang Haoran could not understand why Su Yang uttered the name of the Limitless Sword Sect’s Sect Leader, much less threaten him. However, none of that mattered.

"Did you hear what I said?" Su Yang asked, a faint frown appearing on his face.

Still too frightened to speak, Wang Haoran could only nod his head repeatedly.

Once Su Yang had completely departed and his presence could no longer be felt, Wang Haoran let out a deep sigh and took several long breaths.

"That killing intent... it was absolutely real," he whispered. "Does he bear a grudge against the Limitless Sword Sect?"

His expression twisted with frustration.

"And now he wants me to relay a threat to the Sect Leader? Even as a Core Disciple, meeting him isn’t that simple! Damn it... I’m not setting foot in this place again!"

With that resolve, Wang Haoran turned around and fled in the opposite direction from where Su Yang went, quickly leaving the Boundless Yin Yang Sect.

Meanwhile, Lin Xinyi stared at Su Yang’s back with a look of awe on her face.

’He really defeated a Core Disciple from the Limitless Sword Sect... and so easily, too.’

Since she couldn’t sense Sword Will, she wasn’t able to witness the whole thing. However, even without needing to see it, she could tell that Su Yang had overwhelmed his opponent.

’What is someone so talented with the sword doing in the Boundless Yin Yang Sect?’ she couldn’t help but wonder.

Some time later, they arrived at their destination.

Unsurprisingly, their arrival immediately drew the attention of everyone present.

By the time they arrived, nearly a dozen people were already gathered there. Lin Xinyi didn’t recognize any of them, but the robes they wore told her everything.

They were Sect Elders.

"What are you doing here? Ordinary disciples are prohibited from entering this section of the sect, much less this place!" One of the Sect Elders approached them and said.

"I am Disciple Xiao Yang, and she is my partner, Disciple Lin Xinyi. We’re here to experience the Eternal Yin Yang Caves."

The entire place fell into a deathly silence as every Sect Elder present stared at Su Yang, their faces frozen in speechless disbelief.

"Disciple Xiao Yang? Oh, right. You’re the one at the Dual Cultivation Exchange."

They understood why Su Yang would be there, but an Outer Court disciple like Lin Xinyi participating in the Eternal Yin Yang Caves? A privilege reserved only for the most celebrated Sect Elders in the sect and the top three disciples?

Even as a joke, it wouldn’t be funny.

The Sect Elder standing before them trembled slightly before speaking to Lin Xinyi, "I will pretend I didn’t see you, so turn around and get lost before I—"

"Stop it." A calm voice suddenly interrupted.

The next moment, a beautiful female Sect Elder descended before them and said, "The Sect Leader has approved of their participation in this year’s Eternal Yin Yang Cave."

"Elder Chu! Are you serious?"

"There’s no way! Why would the Sect Leader allow this?! I understand why Xiao Yang is allowed to participate, but this Outer Court Disciple?! Why?!"

"I don’t know the details, but you can ask the Sect Leader when she arrives later."

After directing all their questions to the Sect Leader, the Sect Elders fell silent again.

A few minutes later, another individual descended onto the peak.

"Y-You! What the hell are you doing here?!"

Su Yang turned to look at the newcomer.

It was a familiar face.

"Hey, Vice Sect Leader." Su Yang casually greeted Chen Yi. "Why wouldn’t I be here? I am the one and only reason we won the Dual Cultivation Exchange against the Mystic Delight Pavilion."

"That’s..." Chen Yi began, quickly realizing that Su Yang was right and that he had spoken too hastily.

His gaze then shifted, landing on Lin Xinyi, who stood behind Su Yang like a timid animal trying to hide from attention.

"An Outer Court disciple? Who are you and what are you doing here?" Chen Yi immediately changed his target.

"She’s my partner for the Eternal Yin Yang Cave," Su Yang explained. "I have already received the Sect Leader’s approval, so if you have any complaints, please direct them toward her."

Chen Yi trembled with anger at the sheer lack of respect Su Yang was showing him. However, before he could say anything, another voice suddenly resounded.

"Who has complaints for me?"

Sect Leader Qing appeared before them the next moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 1145: Qualifications
chapters.body:
Chapter 1145: Qualifications

Chapter 1145: Qualifications

After landing on the ground, the Sect Leader swept the scene with a calm gaze.

"Sect Leader, is it true that you allowed this Outer Court disciple to participate in the Eternal Yin Yang Cave?" Chen Yi asked her.

Sect Leader Qing looked at Chen Yi before turning to look at Lin Xinyi and Su Yang, who calmly looked back with a smile.

Sect Leader Qing sighed inwardly before nodding her head, "Yes. Do you have a problem with that?"

"O-Of course, I do!" Chen Yi hesitated for a split second but didn’t back down and argued. "I understand why you’d let Disciple Xiao participate, but an Outer Court disciple who only recently joined the sect without any significant achievements? That’s a slap in the face to everyone who poured sweat and blood to be here! Isn’t that right, everyone?!"

Chen Yi said, looking at the other Sect Elders.

"Vice Sect Leader is right. Sect Leader."

After a moment of silence, one of the Sect Elders spoke up.

"I have no issue with Disciple Xiao Yang being here, but the Outer Court disciple... It’s too much."

"I also agree with the Vice Sect Leader. A disciple with no achievements does not belong here, much less an Outer Court disciple who only recently joined the sect."

Hearing their justifiable complaints, Sect Leader Qing fell silent for a moment before turning to look at Su Yang.

"What do you have to say about this, Disciple Xiao?" She decided to push the decision to him, as he was the one who brought Lin Xinyi there.

Before Su Yang could even respond, Lin Xinyi stepped forward and spoke with her head lowered. "The Seniors are right. I have no qualifications to be here, so I will leave immediately."

Although she felt humiliated, Lin Xinyi did not complain and quietly accepted reality. Even before arriving, she knew it was probably too good to be true, so she wasn’t surprised by the outcome. In the end, it wasn’t entirely unreasonable.

Just as Lin Xinyi turned around and started leaving, Su Yang’s voice resounded loud and clear, "Where are you going?"

Lin Xinyi halted her steps and turned to look at him with raised eyebrows, wondering if he’d somehow not heard the Sect Elders telling her to leave.

"I don’t know what you’re trying to do, Disciple Xiao, but it would be wise to drop this matter before you lose your qualifications as well," Chen Yi said suddenly.

Su Yang turned to stare him in the eyes before speaking in a calm voice, "If we’re going to talk about qualifications, then in my eyes, none of you are qualified to be here."

"What?! The audacity!"

"Watch your mouth! Do you even understand what you’re saying?!"

Chen Yi and the other Sect Elders were instantly enraged by his words. For a mere junior to question their qualifications was simply outrageous.

Su Yang’s demeanor remained unchanged as he explained, "Think about it. What have any of you actually done for this sect that qualifies you to cultivate in the Eternal Yin Yang Cave? In my eyes, all you’ve done is drain the sect of its precious resources without contributing anything in return. If you had truly contributed to this sect, this place wouldn’t have declined to such a state and become the laughingstock of others."

"How dare you spout such nonsense!"

"You’ve overstepped your bounds as a disciple, Xiao Yang!"

"And to say this in front of the Sect Leader?!"

The Sect Elders erupted in anger, their fury fueled largely by the humiliation of being rebuked in front of the Sect Leader.

Su Yang shrugged. "Am I wrong, though? If it weren’t for me, the Boundless Yin Yang Sect would have become an even bigger laughingstock after being completely overwhelmed by the Mystic Delight Pavilion. Because of me, the sect’s reputation hasn’t been tarnished for once in a long time, even improving it."

"So in my eyes, all of you here—who haven’t done anything worth celebrating—have no more qualifications than my partner, an Outer Court disciple."

"That’s enough! I can no longer tolerate your blatant disrespect! I must discipline you here and now before you become truly irredeemable!" Chen Yi shouted as a powerful aura erupted from his body.

However, just as Chen Yi abruptly released his cultivation, it was suppressed by an even stronger pressure.

"Enough!" Sect Leader Qing raised her voice, instantly silencing the group.

"Sect Leader... don’t tell me you’re going to defend him? I know he saved our faces with the Mystic Delight Pavilion, but there’s a limit to it! If we let a mere disciple walk all over us, our entire sect will collapse!" Chen Yi said.

Sect Leader Qing closed her eyes and took a deep breath before speaking, "We can continue this conversation later—after the Eternal Yin Yang Cave. I don’t have the time for this nonsense right now."

"B-But Sect Leader!"

"The next person to utter another word will not be allowed to enter the Eternal Yin Yang Cave," she declared in a stern voice.

Chen Yi opened his mouth to quickly close it again without uttering a sound.

Seeing how adamant the Sect Leader was about ending the matter there, Chen Yi had no choice but to accept it. However, the anger in his heart remained, and he made sure Su Yang felt it as he stared daggers at him.

Never before had he been disrespected by a mere disciple like this.

Su Yang returned the stare with a calm gaze, almost as if he wasn’t intimidated by it. Then, without saying a word, grabbed Lin Xinyi’s hand and pulled her back to his side.

Lin Xinyi wanted to say something, but found herself utterly speechless. She turned to look at the Sect Leader, who wasn’t paying attention to her but instead staring at the entrance to the Eternal Yin Yang Cave.

’Am I really allowed to be here?’ she wondered inwardly.

Suddenly, Su Yang’s voice resounded in her head, "Don’t overthink it and just follow me."

Hearing this, Lin Xinyi could only silently nod her head.
